text:
is a character and the tritagonist in the movie Fullmetal Alchemist: The Sacred Star of Milos. She was featured on the front page of the movie website, holding a rifle and wearing Edward Elric's red jacket. Julia is seen again in the third teaser trailer, wearing a belted jacket and red headband and firing a similar weapon. Julia is a descendant of Milos, and is the younger sister of Ashleigh Crichton.

History

Julia was relocated to Creta with her brother Ashleigh and her parents when she was young. Her parents were alchemists, and Ashleigh would read through their notes. One night though, their family is attacked, and their parents are killed. When Ashleigh went to investigate and found their parents murdered, he too was attacked and Julia passed out. She was unconscious as she was carried away from the mansion where they had lived in Creta. She was eventually found by the people who were for the restoration of Milos and eventually formed a friendship with a woman who was part of the Black Bats and named Miranda. She eventually gets arrested for entering Amestris illegally and is freed by an attack that came on the immigrant compound from Atlas while he was disguised as Ashleigh. She is picked up by Miranda but gets separated again and Alphonse Elric ends up breaking her fall before the both of them ended up tumbling down the cliff edge into the home of those for the return of Milos. Atlas (as Ashleigh) convinces her that he is Ashleigh and begins to try and manipulate her. Eventually, wolf chimeras emerge and find them. She did not know it at the time, but her real brother was the one sending them to check in on her. She as well as some of the Black Bats and the Elric brothers discuss the 'Sanguine Stone' which is a philosopher's stone. She agrees to help Miranda get the sanguine stone. They come up with a plan to attack Table City and find where the sanguine stone is being hidden. However, during this attack, she is with Atlas still disguised as Ashleigh, who then kills Miranda and has her blood spill into a funnel that goes below the city into a complex network of pipes that lead to the direct location that a sanguine stone will be created. He then kills one of his own allies as Julia watched on in horror. She then gets attacked by Atlas, who had revealed his true identity and the fact that he carved off Ashleigh's face to take his persona in order to trick her all of those years later so that he could get the alchemic circle that her parents had tattooed onto her body, as he had also removed the skin from Ashleigh where his circle had lied, and he combines the two circles to activate the process of creating the sanguine stone, which he wants for himself. He is about to kill her, but then the Elric brothers show up just in time. She is given Edward's coat to cover her ripped top. The three of them then travel back to the people as an explosion had occurred in table city and had set off a cascade of events that now had lava coming towards her people rapidly. However, they still had to stop Atlas. They confront Atlas and get to the location of the sanguine stone, and Julia gets her hands on it and is overwhelmed by the power she feels when she grips it. She swallows the stone and attacks Atlas. However, during the fight with Atlas, the true Ashleigh shows up and is the one to deal the killing blow to Atlas. He then tries to convince Julia to join his side and be a Cretan. She refuses and Ashleigh begins to fight her. She and Alphonse are behind a wall of rock as lava closes in on them. Julia's power with the stone was not enough alone to stop the lava. She and Ashleigh fight directly and in the process, she is able to overwhelm Ashleigh's power and both she and Ashleigh's matching jewelry get broken. However, with the Elric brothers and Mustang's help, they are able to stop the flow of lava and save the people. After the fight, Ashleigh is near death, and despite the Elrics trying to stop her, she uses the sanguine stone to heal her brother and keep him from dying. She passes out and coughs up the remains of the stone along with blood just after Ashleigh does the same. She later wakes up in a hospital room, finding that her earring had been fixed and that the matching item had been taken and she smiled. She later goes out to meet one more time, now sporting a new prosthetic leg, with Ed and Al before they leave the now reclaimed Milos.

Personality

Julia is a kind, strong-willed young woman who would do anything to restore the former glory of her nation, and desperately seeks great power to do so. She, however, disapproves of anything that is immoral and unethical such as sacrificing humans for the Philosopher's Stone. She dearly loves her brother Ashleigh and seeks nothing more than to be with him after the conflict, and fights him, albeit reluctantly.

Alchemy & Abilities

Julia's signature alchemy revolves on healing. She's capable albeit with some difficulty to heal small wounds. The transmutation circle used for this is imprinted on paper and is composed of two greater circles, one small circle in the center with a spiral. From the middle circle, 3 ovals with a line in the middle are imprinted, one of them with 3 additional circles within and one of them with the same spiral, with the outer rings of the transmutation circle, enveloping a triangle with two circles in one point.

While in the possession of the Philosopher's Stone, she can perform incredibly powerful alchemy. Similar to Atlas, she can release lightning albeit far more powerful than his as well as using water and ice. Also she, with much effort, is able to manipulate large portions of the Earth to stop flowing magma that even Al could not stop with walls. Her healing ability is also greatly enhanced to the point Ashleigh's ripped skin was repaired. After losing the stone and passing through the gate of truth to save the life of her brother, Julia presumably gained the ability to use alchemy without the use of transmutation circles at the end of the movie, though it is never demonstrated.

Contrary to appearances she's quite athletic and could easily jump out of a building with careful but fast acrobatic jumping in lower floors, to ensure a safe landing. She also knows how to wield and shoot a rifle and other firearms with relative proficiency, as well as use the equipment of the Black Bats to fly.

Trivia

Julia follows the trend of non-canon female Fullmetal Alchemist characters in protagonist roles being given red or orange hair, being the fourth following Armony Eiselstein, Corniche Royce, and Viola Amore.
Julia is the first character of this group to be shown purely in animated form rather than in a video game.
Her seiyuu, Maaya Sakamoto, also voiced Sophie Bergmann in the video game Fullmetal Alchemist 3: The Girl Who Surpasses God.
Interestingly, Julia also utters the phrase "It's not a question of whether we can, it's something we must do". Sakamoto has uttered this line before as the voice of Final Fantasy XIII 's protagonist, Lightning.
Because she opened the Gate of Truth to prevent her brother from dying, Julia qualifies as a potential Human Sacrifice, except for the fact that her character was created long after the series had already ended. Storyline-wise it can be posited that Roy Mustang and company, having seen plenty of suspicious things from their own military (including Peter Soyuz's occupation of Table City), kept quiet about this to protect Milos.
She plays a potential love-interest for Alphonse.
Julia lost her leg while saving her brother, a similar situation is with Edward losing his arm to save his brother.
